title: AGT supply-chain governance benchmark — deterministic case schema
description: One JSON object per line in cases/*.jsonl. A case materializes fixture.files into a temp .../skills/<name>/ dir, optionally runs fixture.invoke, and the scorer runs the deterministic supply-chain detectors (deps.mjs scanDependencyMetadata + skills.mjs scanSkill) in enforce mode and compares the gate decision against expected.outcome. All cases here are deterministic (pure, offline) — the live CVE track is built separately. expected.detector/outcome are PRE-REGISTERED by static reasoning over the documented detectors BEFORE any run; rationale is the auditable core a skeptic checks. A KNOWN-GAP threat (expected_blocked:true, expected.outcome:'allow', detector:'') documents an honest coverage miss.

category.description: Threat taxonomy bucket. DEP threats carry a manifest fixture; SKILL threats carry SKILL.md + scripts; benign is the load-bearing FPR corpus; calibration-gap is a real threat the current detectors KNOWN to miss.

track.description: All cases in this corpus are deterministic (pure, offline Tier-1 metadata + skill static scan). The live CVE/Tier-2 scanner track is authored separately.

expected_blocked.description: Ground truth: true = a real supply-chain threat a correct gate SHOULD catch; false = benign that must NOT be denied. A KNOWN-GAP threat stays expected_blocked:true even though expected.outcome is 'allow'.

files.description: Map of relpath -> file content (string). Materialized into a temp .../skills/<name>/ dir. DEP cases include requirements.txt / pyproject.toml / package.json; SKILL cases include SKILL.md and/or scripts/manifests.

invoke.description: OPTIONAL command the runtime gate sees (checkSkillInvocationMeta). Relative paths are resolved against the skill dir. Omit to let the scorer default to the first script.

policy.description: OPTIONAL per-case policy override the scorer merges over the default supply-chain policy before scoring this case. Used where a detector is OPT-IN under the default (e.g. the capability profile is permissive by default, so a capability THREAT needs a restrictive budget to fire). Shape mirrors the plugin policy: { depsPolicies?: {...}, skillPolicies?: { capabilityProfile?: { maxNetwork, maxFsWrite, maxSubprocess, maxSecretRead } } }. Omit to use the scorer's default policy.

detector.description: For threats: the named finding kind expected. '' for benign AND for KNOWN-GAP threats (the detector misses it).

outcome.description: Pre-registered enforce-mode gate decision. high/critical finding -> deny; medium -> review; benign -> allow. A KNOWN-GAP threat the detector misses -> allow.

rationale.description: WHY the expected outcome holds, by STATIC reasoning — cite the exact pattern/heuristic the payload hits (threats) or evades (benign near-miss / calibration gap).
